name: Johnson & Bryan Law Firm PLLC
description_short: Texas-based law firm specializing in debt litigation defense, settlement negotiation, and bankruptcy filing for consumers facing creditor lawsuits and collection actions.
description_long:
Johnson & Bryan is a debt relief law firm based in Houston, Texas, founded to help consumers throughout Texas navigate complex debt collection and creditor litigation. The firm positions itself as a defensive advocate against aggressive collection tactics by banks, finance companies, and debt buyers who file lawsuits seeking default judgments.

The firm offers multiple debt resolution pathways including debt settlement negotiation, bankruptcy filing assistance (Chapter 7 and Chapter 13), litigation defense against creditor lawsuits, judgment enforcement defense, lien removal assistance, and creditor harassment cessation. They handle cases involving credit card debt, debt buyer accounts, private student loans, medical bills, auto/vehicle loans, and personal guarantee obligations. The firm advertises handling of specific creditor categories including American Express, Chase Bank, National Collegiate Student Loans, and Midland Funding.

Johnson & Bryan distinguishes itself through litigation-focused expertise rather than settlement-only models, claiming to have defended thousands of clients and highlighting specific trial wins with documented debt elimination outcomes ($35K-$122K per case cited). Their messaging emphasizes knowledge of Texas debt collection law loopholes and technical defenses, positioning the firm as specialized in countering aggressive collection tactics rather than proactive credit management.

The firm's model is contingent on litigation outcomes and client debt elimination success. However, their website displays unverified statistics ("99.99% of Debt Repaid," "$0 Amount Saved" placeholder metrics) and limited transparency on fee structures, service costs, or realistic case outcome percentages. While case examples are specific, the small sample size and selection bias toward major victories suggests these may not be representative of typical client results.

Consumers considering bankruptcy should also explore alternatives. Debt relief programs may negotiate settlements for less than owed, while debt consolidation loans can simplify payments. Credit counseling agencies offer free financial assessments. After bankruptcy, rebuilding credit through secured credit cards and credit builder loans provides a structured path back. Credit repair services can help ensure accurate reporting. After discharge, qualifying for an installment loan can begin rebuilding payment history on your credit report.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Litigation-focused approach with specific trial wins against major creditors (American Express, Chase, Midland Funding, National Collegiate Student Loans)
Handles multiple debt types including the difficult categories of debt buyer accounts and private student loans
Texas-specific expertise in debt collection law technicalities and legal defenses
Addresses creditor harassment and helps clients with liens, judgments, and bank account freezes
Offers bankruptcy filing assistance alongside litigation defense, providing multiple resolution pathways
Addresses cases at various stages (pre-lawsuit, active litigation, post-judgment)
Explicitly targets consumers overwhelmed by collection efforts and facing legal action
cons: Website displays placeholder/unverified metrics ("$0 Amount Saved by our Clients," "99.99% Percentage of Debt Repaid") undermining credibility
No transparent fee structure, cost disclosure, or payment model clearly stated on website
Limited information on success rates, typical outcomes, or what percentage of cases result in debt elimination vs. reduced payments
Only 4 case examples provided with no timeline, sample size data, or indication of typical client results
Heavy marketing language and emotional appeals ("greedy creditors," "brink of financial ruin") rather than balanced client education
best_for: Texas consumers actively being sued by creditors or debt collectors seeking immediate legal defense
Individuals with debt buyer accounts or debts sold to collection agencies pursuing litigation
Borrowers with private student loans and medical debt who need litigation defense options
Consumers with existing judgments, liens, or frozen bank accounts needing enforcement defense
diagnosis: Best for Texas residents actively facing creditor lawsuits or debt buyer litigation who need immediate legal defense rather than preventive credit management. Primary caveat: website credibility is undermined by unverified metrics and placeholder statistics; potential clients should request transparent fee agreements, realistic success rate data, and case outcome details before engagement.
services: Debt lawsuit defense and litigation representation
Debt settlement negotiation with creditors and debt buyers
Home lien removal and enforcement defense
Creditor harassment cessation and FDCPA violation defense
Debt buyer account defense and dispute
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
address: 701 N Post Oak Rd #140, Houston, TX 77024
